'''田遊び'''（たあそび） *[[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[板橋の田遊び]]を参照。 *[[静岡県]][[志太郡]][[大井川町]]字藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[藤守の田遊び]]を参照。 *静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[滝沢の田遊び]]を参照。

'''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
*[[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[板橋の田遊び]]を参照。 *[[静岡県]][[志太郡]][[大井川町]]字藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[藤守の田遊び]]を参照。 *静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[滝沢の田遊び]]を参照。
{{aimai}} [[Category:予祝芸能|たあそひ]] [[Category:日本の伝統芸能|たあそひ]]

'''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
*[[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[板橋の田遊び]]を参照。 *[[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[藤守の田遊び]]を参照。 *静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[滝沢の田遊び]]を参照。
{{aimai}} [[Category:予祝芸能|たあそひ]] [[Category:日本の伝統芸能|たあそひ]]

*[[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[板橋の田遊び]]を参照。 *[[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[藤守の田遊び]]を参照。 *静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[滝沢の田遊び]]を参照。
{{aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:たあそひ}} [[Category:予祝芸能] [[Category:日本の伝統芸能]]

'''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
*[[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[板橋の田遊び]]を参照。 *[[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能。[[藤守の田遊び]]を参照。 *静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[滝沢の田遊び]]を参照。 *静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能。[[小國神社の田遊び]]を参照。
{{aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:たあそひ}} [[Category:予祝芸能] [[Category:日本の伝統芸能]]

'''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
== 概要 == 田遊びは、稲作の作業次第を模擬的に演じてその年の[[豊作]]を祈願する[[予祝芸能|予祝]]行事で、[[正月|新春]]に行われる場合が多い。田遊びという呼称は主に[[東海地方]]などで使用され、[[御田]]・[[御田植祭]]・春鍬・春田打ちなどとも称する。地方によって違いはあるが、中心は種まきから刈取りまでを演じる。
例えば、[[東京都]][[板橋区]][[徳丸]]本町の北野天神社で、毎年[[2月21日]]に行われる「徳丸の田遊び」は、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』によると「田遊び祭と云神事あり」と記されている。田遊びは[[神社]][[境内]]に設けられたモガリと呼ばれる2[[間]]四方の場所で、稲作の作業次第を模擬し演じられる。なかでも[[農民]]たちの昼休みである「呼び込み」では、予祝を意図して安女と太郎次が抱き合って舞い、性的所作を演じる。
この他、同様な行事として、[[風流踊|風流化]]された[[東北地方]]の[[田植踊]]や[[八戸のえんぶり]]などもある。
== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁
{{日本の伝統芸能}}
{{デフォルトソート:たあそひ}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:神道]] [[Category:予祝芸能]] {{Event-stub}}

revision.comment: 概要に追記し、概要から事例節を分割。脚注節を追加。-{{stub}}
悪戯を差し戻して微編集
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019-09-22}} '''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
== 概要 == 田遊びは、稲作の作業次第を模擬的に演じてその年の[[豊作]]を祈願する[[予祝芸能|予祝]]行事で、[[正月|新春]]に行われる場合が多い。田遊びという呼称は主に[[東海地方]]などで使用され、[[御田]]・[[御田植祭]]・春鍬・春田打ちなどとも称する。地方によって違いはあるが、中心は種まきから刈取りまでを演じる。 [[新井恒易]]によれば、全国で300余りの伝承があるという。
多くの田遊びで[[ウシ]]（もしくは[[ウマ]]）による耕作の場があり、見せ場となっている{{sfn|星野 |1990|pp=199-208}}。人間がホロを被ってウシに扮し[[犂]]などを牽いて[[田起こし]]や[[代かき]]を象ってみせる。牛の[[人形]]などの代替物で表現する場合もある。中にはウシが暴れる展開となるものや、ウシを褒めて労う演出のものなど様々である。[[折口信夫]]は『田遊び祭りの概念』の中で、田遊びの牛は本来は[[田の神]]への[[wikt:贄|犠]]であり、それでもって田の豊穣を祈願したのではなかったか、と述べている。
== 事例 == 例えば、[[東京都]][[板橋区]][[徳丸]]本町の北野天神社で、毎年[[2月21日]]に行われる「徳丸の田遊び」は、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』によると「田遊び祭と云神事あり」と記されている。田遊びは[[神社]][[境内]]に設けられたモガリと呼ばれる2[[間]]四方の場所で、稲作の作業次第を模擬し演じられる。なかでも[[農民]]たちの昼休みである「呼び込み」では、予祝を意図して安女と太郎次が抱き合って舞い、性的所作を演じる。
この他、同様な行事として、[[風流踊|風流化]]された[[東北地方]]の[[田植踊]]や[[八戸のえんぶり]]などもある。
== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = [[星野紘]] |title = 音楽的呪から身体演技へ |journal = 身ぶりと音楽 |date = 1990 |publisher = 東京書籍 |series = 民族音楽叢書 |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }}
{{日本の伝統芸能}} {{デフォルトソート:たあそひ}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:神道]] [[Category:予祝芸能]]

この他、同様な行事として、[[風流踊|風流化]]された[[東北地方]]の[[田植踊]]や[[八戸のえんぶり]]などもある。
== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。

== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = [[星野紘]] |title = 音楽的呪から身体演技へ |journal = 身ぶりと音楽 |date = 1990 |publisher = 東京書籍 |series = 民族音楽叢書 |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }}

{{出典の明記|date=2019-09-22}} '''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
== 概要 == 田遊びは、稲作の作業次第を模擬的に演じてその年の[[豊作]]を祈願する[[予祝芸能|予祝]]行事で、[[正月|新春]]に行われる場合が多い。田遊びという呼称は主に[[東海地方]]などで使用され、[[御田]]・[[御田植祭]]・春鍬・春田打ちなどとも称する。地方によって違いはあるが、中心は種まきから刈取りまでを演じる。 [[新井恒易]]によれば、全国で300余りの伝承があるという。
多くの田遊びで[[ウシ]]（もしくは[[ウマ]]）による耕作の場があり、見せ場となっている{{sfn|星野 |1990|pp=199-208}}。人間がホロを被ってウシに扮し[[犂]]などを牽いて[[田起こし]]や[[代かき]]を象ってみせる。牛の[[人形]]などの代替物で表現する場合もある。中にはウシが暴れる展開となるものや、ウシを褒めて労う演出のものなど様々である。[[折口信夫]]は『田遊び祭りの概念』の中で、田遊びの牛は本来は[[田の神]]への[[wikt:贄|犠]]であり、それでもって田の豊穣を祈願したのではなかったか、と述べている。
== 事例 == 例えば、[[東京都]][[板橋区]][[徳丸]]本町の北野天神社で、毎年[[2月21日]]に行われる「徳丸の田遊び」は、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』によると「田遊び祭と云神事あり」と記されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}。田遊びは[[神社]][[境内]]に設けられたモガリと呼ばれる2[[間]]四方の場所で、稲作の作業次第を模擬し演じられる。なかでも[[農民]]たちの昼休みである「呼び込み」では、予祝を意図して安女と太郎次が抱き合って舞い、性的所作を演じる。
この他、同様な行事として、[[風流踊|風流化]]された[[東北地方]]の[[田植踊]]や[[八戸のえんぶり]]などもある。
== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = [[星野紘]] |title = 音楽的呪から身体演技へ |journal = 身ぶりと音楽 |date = 1990 |publisher = 東京書籍 |series = 民族音楽叢書 |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ14豊島郡ノ6|chapter=徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}}}
{{日本の伝統芸能}} {{デフォルトソート:たあそひ}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:神道]] [[Category:予祝芸能]]

{{出典の明記|date=2019-09-22}} '''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
== 概要 == 田遊びは、稲作の作業次第を模擬的に演じてその年の[[豊作]]を祈願する[[予祝芸能|予祝]]行事で、[[正月|新春]]に行われる場合が多い。田遊びという呼称は主に[[東海地方]]などで使用され、[[御田]]・[[御田植祭]]・春鍬・春田打ち・田祭り<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html 鶴見の歴史 第5回:鶴見の伝統芸能（その2）鶴見の田祭り]（鶴見区役所）</ref>などとも称する。地方によって違いはあるが、中心は種まきから刈取りまでを演じる。[[新井恒易]]によれば、全国で300余りの伝承があるという。
多くの田遊びで[[ウシ]]（もしくは[[ウマ]]）による耕作の場があり、見せ場となっている{{sfn|星野 |1990|pp=199-208}}。人間がホロを被ってウシに扮し[[犂]]などを牽いて[[田起こし]]や[[代かき]]を象ってみせる。牛の[[人形]]などの代替物で表現する場合もある。中にはウシが暴れる展開となるものや、ウシを褒めて労う演出のものなど様々である。[[折口信夫]]は『田遊び祭りの概念』の中で、田遊びの牛は本来は[[田の神]]への[[wikt:贄|犠]]であり、それでもって田の豊穣を祈願したのではなかったか、と述べている。
== 事例 == 例えば、[[東京都]][[板橋区]][[徳丸]]本町の北野天神社で、毎年[[2月21日]]に行われる「徳丸の田遊び」は、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』によると「田遊び祭と云神事あり」と記されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}。田遊びは[[神社]][[境内]]に設けられたモガリと呼ばれる2[[間]]四方の場所で、稲作の作業次第を模擬し演じられる。なかでも[[農民]]たちの昼休みである「呼び込み」では、予祝を意図して安女と太郎次が抱き合って舞い、性的所作を演じる。
この他、同様な行事として、[[風流踊|風流化]]された[[東北地方]]の[[田植踊]]や[[八戸のえんぶり]]などもある。
== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]][[鶴見中央]]に伝わる[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市登録地域無形民俗文化財]]登録の民俗芸能、[[鶴見神社|鶴見の田祭り]]<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf 市域文化財一覧（PDF版）]（横浜市）</ref>。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = [[星野紘]] |title = 音楽的呪から身体演技へ |journal = 身ぶりと音楽 |date = 1990 |publisher = 東京書籍 |series = 民族音楽叢書 |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ14豊島郡ノ6|chapter=徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}}}

revision.comment: 曖昧さ回避ページ[[鶴見神社]]へのリンクを解消、リンク先を[[鶴見神社 (横浜市)]]に変更([[User:ネイ/DisamAssist|DisamAssist]]使用)
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019-09-22}} '''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
== 概要 == 田遊びは、稲作の作業次第を模擬的に演じてその年の[[豊作]]を祈願する[[予祝芸能|予祝]]行事で、[[正月|新春]]に行われる場合が多い。田遊びという呼称は主に[[東海地方]]などで使用され、[[御田]]・[[御田植祭]]・春鍬・春田打ち・田祭り<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html 鶴見の歴史 第5回:鶴見の伝統芸能（その2）鶴見の田祭り]（鶴見区役所）</ref>などとも称する。地方によって違いはあるが、中心は種まきから刈取りまでを演じる。[[新井恒易]]によれば、全国で300余りの伝承があるという。
多くの田遊びで[[ウシ]]（もしくは[[ウマ]]）による耕作の場があり、見せ場となっている{{sfn|星野 |1990|pp=199-208}}。人間がホロを被ってウシに扮し[[犂]]などを牽いて[[田起こし]]や[[代かき]]を象ってみせる。牛の[[人形]]などの代替物で表現する場合もある。中にはウシが暴れる展開となるものや、ウシを褒めて労う演出のものなど様々である。[[折口信夫]]は『田遊び祭りの概念』の中で、田遊びの牛は本来は[[田の神]]への[[wikt:贄|犠]]であり、それでもって田の豊穣を祈願したのではなかったか、と述べている。
== 事例 == 例えば、[[東京都]][[板橋区]][[徳丸]]本町の北野天神社で、毎年[[2月21日]]に行われる「徳丸の田遊び」は、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』によると「田遊び祭と云神事あり」と記されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}。田遊びは[[神社]][[境内]]に設けられたモガリと呼ばれる2[[間]]四方の場所で、稲作の作業次第を模擬し演じられる。なかでも[[農民]]たちの昼休みである「呼び込み」では、予祝を意図して安女と太郎次が抱き合って舞い、性的所作を演じる。
この他、同様な行事として、[[風流踊|風流化]]された[[東北地方]]の[[田植踊]]や[[八戸のえんぶり]]などもある。
== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]][[鶴見中央]]に伝わる[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市登録地域無形民俗文化財]]登録の民俗芸能、[[鶴見神社 (横浜市)|鶴見の田祭り]]<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf 市域文化財一覧（PDF版）]（横浜市）</ref>。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = [[星野紘]] |title = 音楽的呪から身体演技へ |journal = 身ぶりと音楽 |date = 1990 |publisher = 東京書籍 |series = 民族音楽叢書 |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ14豊島郡ノ6|chapter=徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}}} ==関連項目== * [[志多羅神上洛事件]]
{{日本の伝統芸能}} {{日本の文化財}} {{デフォルトソート:たあそひ}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:神道]] [[Category:予祝芸能]] [[Category:新春の季語]]

revision.text:
'''Ta-asobi''' (田遊び) is a Japanese folk performing art involving the reenactment of [[rice farming]] practices.
== Overview == Ta-asobi is a ritualistic performance that reenacts rice cultivation steps to pray for a [[bountiful harvest]] as part of [[yochū-geinō]] (ritual performance arts for good fortune). It is often held in [[Shinshun]] (early spring). The term "Ta-asobi" is mainly used in the [[Tokai region]], but similar events are referred to as [[Ota]], [[Otaue Matsuri]], Harukuwaki, Haruta-uchi, or Ta-matsuri.<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi, Part 5: Traditional Performing Arts of Tsurumi – Tsurumi's Rice Field Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While there are regional differences, the core involves acting out the process from sowing seeds to harvesting. According to [[Arai Tsuneoyasu]], there are more than 300 traditions of Ta-asobi nationwide.
Many Ta-asobi performances feature scenes of oxen (or sometimes [[horses]]) tilling the fields, which are highlights of the event.<sup>{{sfn|Hoshino|1990|pp=199–208}}</sup> Performers often don ox costumes and mimic plowing with tools like plows. Some use substitutes like ox figurines. Performances range from comedic depictions of unruly oxen to scenes of praise and thanks for the oxen's labor. [[Shinobu Orikuchi]], in his essay *The Concept of Ta-asobi Festivals*, suggested that the ox in Ta-asobi originally symbolized a sacrificial offering to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神}} (deity of the fields) to pray for agricultural abundance.
== Examples == For instance, the "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held annually on [[February 21]] at [[Kitano Tenjinsha]] in [[Tokumaru]] Honcho, [[Itabashi Ward]], Tokyo, has been recorded in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}, which states, "There is a sacred ritual called the Ta-asobi Matsuri."{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Tokumaru Honmura/Tokumaru Wakimura Tenjinsha}} This Ta-asobi takes place in a two-ken square area called "Mogari" within the shrine grounds, where performers act out rice cultivation steps. One notable part is the "Yobikomi," where celebratory dances include explicit gestures, symbolizing fertility, as a man and woman embrace.
Other related rituals include more elaborate versions, such as the {{ill|Rice Planting Dance|ja|田植踊}} of the [[Tohoku region]] and the {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}.
== Notable Ta-asobi Traditions == * [[Itabashi Ward]], Tokyo: {{ill|Itabashi no Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Tsurumi Ward]], Yokohama, Kanagawa: {{ill|Tsurumi no Ta Matsuri|ja|鶴見の田祭り}}, registered as a Yokohama City Intangible Folk Cultural Property.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Citywide Cultural Properties List (PDF)] (Yokohama City)</ref> * [[Yaizu]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Fujimori no Ta-asobi|ja|藤守の田遊び}}, a nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Makinohara]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Hirugaya no Ta-asobi|ja|蛭ヶ谷の田遊び}}, preserved at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}}. * [[Fujieda]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Takizawa no Ta-asobi|ja|滝沢の田遊び}}, recognized as a Shizuoka Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Mishima]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Mishima Taisha no Ota-uchi|ja|三嶋大社のお田打}}, also recognized as a Shizuoka Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Mori Town]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Oguni Shrine no Ta-asobi|ja|小國神社の田遊び}}, a Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * *Dictionary of Regional History*, Vol. 2, Asakura Publishing, 2005, ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043. * {{Cite journal|author=[[Hoshino Hiroshi]]|title=From Musical Incantations to Physical Performance|journal=Gestures and Music|date=1990|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|series=Ethnomusicology Anthology|isbn=4487752590|ref=harv}} * {{cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Honmura/Tokumaru Wakimura Tenjinsha|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|publisher=Interior Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref=sfnref}}
== Related Topics == * [[Shitara no Kami's Journey to Kyoto Incident]]
{{Japan Traditional Performing Arts}} {{Japan Cultural Properties}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ta-asobi}} [[Category:Rice farming]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Ritual performing arts]] [[Category:New Year kigo]] [[ja:田遊び]]

'''Ta-asobi''' (田遊び) is a Japanese folk performing art involving the reenactment of [[rice farming]] practices.
== Overview == Ta-asobi is a ritualistic performance that reenacts rice cultivation steps to pray for a [[bountiful harvest]] as part of [[yochū-geinō]] (ritual performance arts for good fortune). It is often held in [[Shinshun]] (early spring). The term "Ta-asobi" is mainly used in the [[Tokai region]], but similar events are referred to as [[Ota]], [[Otaue Matsuri]], Harukuwaki, Haruta-uchi, or Ta-matsuri.<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi, Part 5: Traditional Performing Arts of Tsurumi – Tsurumi's Rice Field Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While there are regional differences, the core involves acting out the process from sowing seeds to harvesting. According to [[Arai Tsuneoyasu]], there are more than 300 traditions of Ta-asobi nationwide.
Many Ta-asobi performances feature scenes of oxen (or sometimes [[horses]]) tilling the fields, which are highlights of the event.<sup>{{sfn|Hoshino|1990|pp=199–208}}</sup> Performers often don ox costumes and mimic plowing with tools like plows. Some use substitutes like ox figurines. Performances range from comedic depictions of unruly oxen to scenes of praise and thanks for the oxen's labor. [[Shinobu Orikuchi]], in his essay *The Concept of Ta-asobi Festivals*, suggested that the ox in Ta-asobi originally symbolized a sacrificial offering to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神}} (deity of the fields) to pray for agricultural abundance.
== Examples == For instance, the "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held annually on [[February 21]] at [[Kitano Tenjinsha]] in [[Tokumaru]] Honcho, [[Itabashi Ward]], Tokyo, has been recorded in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}, which states, "There is a sacred ritual called the Ta-asobi Matsuri."{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Tokumaru Honmura/Tokumaru Wakimura Tenjinsha}} This Ta-asobi takes place in a two-ken square area called "Mogari" within the shrine grounds, where performers act out rice cultivation steps. One notable part is the "Yobikomi," where celebratory dances include explicit gestures, symbolizing fertility, as a man and woman embrace.
Other related rituals include more elaborate versions, such as the {{ill|Rice Planting Dance|ja|田植踊}} of the [[Tohoku region]] and the {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}.
== Notable Ta-asobi Traditions == * [[Itabashi Ward]], Tokyo: {{ill|Itabashi no Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Tsurumi Ward]], Yokohama, Kanagawa: {{ill|Tsurumi no Ta Matsuri|ja|鶴見の田祭り}}, registered as a Yokohama City Intangible Folk Cultural Property.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Citywide Cultural Properties List (PDF)] (Yokohama City)</ref> * [[Yaizu]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Fujimori no Ta-asobi|ja|藤守の田遊び}}, a nationally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Makinohara]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Hirugaya no Ta-asobi|ja|蛭ヶ谷の田遊び}}, preserved at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}}. * [[Fujieda]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Takizawa no Ta-asobi|ja|滝沢の田遊び}}, recognized as a Shizuoka Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Mishima]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Mishima Taisha no Ota-uchi|ja|三嶋大社のお田打}}, also recognized as a Shizuoka Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * [[Mori Town]], Shizuoka: {{ill|Oguni Shrine no Ta-asobi|ja|小國神社の田遊び}}, a Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property.

'''Ta-asobi''' (田遊び) is a Japanese folk performing art involving the reenactment of [[rice farming]] practices.
== Overview == Ta-asobi is a ritualistic performance that reenacts rice cultivation steps to pray for a [[bountiful harvest]] as part of [[yochū-geinō]] (ritual performance arts for good fortune). It is often held in [[Shinshun]] (early spring). The term "Ta-asobi" is mainly used in the [[Tokai region]], but similar events are referred to as [[Ota]], [[Otaue Matsuri]], Harukuwaki, Haruta-uchi, or Ta-matsuri.<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi, Part 5: Traditional Performing Arts of Tsurumi – Tsurumi's Rice Field Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While there are regional differences, the core involves acting out the process from sowing seeds to harvesting. According to [[Arai Tsuneoyasu]], there are more than 300 traditions of Ta-asobi nationwide.
Many Ta-asobi performances feature scenes of oxen (or sometimes [[horses]]) tilling the fields, which are highlights of the event.<sup>{{sfn|Hoshino|1990|pp=199–208}}</sup> Performers often don ox costumes and mimic plowing with tools like plows. Some use substitutes like ox figurines. Performances range from comedic depictions of unruly oxen to scenes of praise and thanks for the oxen's labor. [[Shinobu Orikuchi]], in his essay *The Concept of Ta-asobi Festivals*, suggested that the ox in Ta-asobi originally symbolized a sacrificial offering to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神}} (deity of the fields) to pray for agricultural abundance.

One notable part is the "Yobikomi," where celebratory dances include explicit gestures, symbolizing fertility, as a man and woman embrace.
Other related rituals include more elaborate versions, such as the {{ill|Rice Planting Dance|ja|田植踊}} of the [[Tohoku region]] and the {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}.

'''Ta-asobi''' (田遊び) is a Japanese folk performing art involving the reenactment of [[rice farming]] practices.

It is often held in [[Shinshun]] (early spring). [[Shinobu Orikuchi]], in his essay *The Concept of Ta-asobi Festivals*, suggested that the ox in Ta-asobi originally symbolized a sacrificial offering to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|ja|田の神}} (deity of the fields) to pray for agricultural abundance.

One notable part is the "Yobikomi," where celebratory dances include explicit gestures, symbolizing fertility, as a man and woman embrace.
Other related rituals include more elaborate versions, such as the {{ill|Rice Planting Dance|ja|田植踊}} of the [[Tohoku region]] and the {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}}.

{{Japan Traditional Performing Arts}} {{Japan Cultural Properties}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ta-asobi}} [[Category:Rice farming]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Ritual performing arts]] [[Category:New Year kigo]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:田遊び]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Festival|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

[[Category:Rice cultivation]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Ritual performances]] [[Category:New Year seasonal terms]] [[ja:田遊び]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}

[[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Rice cultivation]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Ritual performances]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:New Year seasonal terms]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:New pages]] [[ja:田遊び]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件}}

* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

[[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Rice cultivation]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Ritual performances]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:New Year seasonal terms]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Ta-asobi-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[ja:田遊び]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Rice cultivation]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Ritual performances]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:New Year seasonal terms]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ta-asobi-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:田遊び]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}

During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

[[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Rice cultivation]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Ritual performances]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:New Year seasonal terms]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ta-asobi-qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:田遊び]] [[ja:Ta-asobi]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件}}
