revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated start]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件}}
{{ill|Traditional Japanese Arts|ja|日本の伝統芸能}} {{ill|Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|日本の文化財}}

Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件}}

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.

[[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ta-asobi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Rice cultivation]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ta-asobi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ta-asobi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ritual performances]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ta-asobi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New Year seasonal terms]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ta-asobi}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] <!--Ta-asobi-->[[Category:qq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:田遊び]] [[sn:a-asobi]] [[Category:New]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]] <!--Ta-asobi-->[[Category:11]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Ta-asobi]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ta-asobi]][[Category:11]] [[ja:田遊び]] [[sn:a-asobi]] [[Category:New]]

'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}

== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件}}

According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.

For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日|12=draft}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|12=draft}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=draft}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|12=draft}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方|12=draft}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘|12=draft}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件|12=draft}}
{{ill|Traditional Japanese Arts|ja|日本の伝統芸能|12=draft}} {{ill|Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|日本の文化財|12=draft}} <!--Ta-asobi-->

revision.text:
'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|12=draft}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技|12=draft}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=draft}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能|12=draft}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|ritual|ja|儀式|12=draft}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|bountiful harvest|ja|豊作|12=draft}}. It is often held during the {{ill|New Year|ja|正月|12=draft}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=draft}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬|12=draft}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち|12=draft}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り|12=draft}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易|12=draft}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|oxen|ja|ウシ|12=draft}} (or {{ill|horses|ja|ウマ|12=draft}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂|12=draft}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし|12=draft}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき|12=draft}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Nobuo Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫|12=draft}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|rice field deities|ja|田の神|12=draft}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|ja|2月21日|12=draft}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|12=draft}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=draft}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|12=draft}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tohoku|ja|東北地方|12=draft}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of {{ill|Itabashi Ward|ja|板橋区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu City|ja|焼津市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Makinohara City|ja|牧之原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda City|ja|藤枝市|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|ja|三嶋大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mishima City|ja|三島市|12=draft}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, designated as a {{ill|Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘|12=draft}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件|12=draft}}
{{ill|Traditional Japanese Arts|ja|日本の伝統芸能|12=draft}} {{ill|Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|日本の文化財|12=draft}} <!--Ta-asobi-->

revision.text:
'''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
== 概要 == 田遊びは、稲作の作業次第を模擬的に演じてその年の[[豊作]]を祈願する[[予祝芸能|予祝]]行事で、[[正月|新春]]に行われる場合が多い。田遊びという呼称は主に[[東海地方]]などで使用され、[[御田]]・[[御田植祭]]・春鍬・春田打ち・田祭り<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html 鶴見の歴史 第5回:鶴見の伝統芸能（その2）鶴見の田祭り]（鶴見区役所）</ref>などとも称する。地方によって違いはあるが、中心は種まきから刈取りまでを演じる。[[新井恒易]]によれば、全国で300余りの伝承があるという。
多くの田遊びで[[ウシ]]（もしくは[[ウマ]]）による耕作の場があり、見せ場となっている{{sfn|星野 |1990|pp=199-208}}。人間がホロを被ってウシに扮し[[犂]]などを牽いて[[田起こし]]や[[代かき]]を象ってみせる。牛の[[人形]]などの代替物で表現する場合もある。中にはウシが暴れる展開となるものや、ウシを褒めて労う演出のものなど様々である。[[折口信夫]]は『田遊び祭りの概念』の中で、田遊びの牛は本来は[[田の神]]への[[wikt:贄|犠]]であり、それでもって田の豊穣を祈願したのではなかったか、と述べている。
== 事例 == 例えば、[[東京都]][[板橋区]][[徳丸 (板橋区)|徳丸]]本町の北野天神社で、毎年[[2月21日]]に行われる「徳丸の田遊び」は、『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』によると「田遊び祭と云神事あり」と記されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}。田遊びは[[神社]][[境内]]に設けられたモガリと呼ばれる2[[間]]四方の場所で、稲作の作業次第を模擬し演じられる。なかでも[[農民]]たちの昼休みである「呼び込み」では、予祝を意図して安女と太郎次が抱き合って舞い、性的所作を演じる。
この他、同様な行事として、[[風流踊|風流化]]された[[東北地方]]の[[田植踊]]や[[八戸のえんぶり]]などもある。
== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]][[鶴見中央]]に伝わる[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市登録地域無形民俗文化財]]登録の民俗芸能、[[鶴見神社 (横浜市)|鶴見の田祭り]]<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf 市域文化財一覧（PDF版）]（横浜市）</ref>。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = [[星野紘]] |title = 音楽的呪から身体演技へ |journal = 身ぶりと音楽 |date = 1990 |publisher = 東京書籍 |series = 民族音楽叢書 |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ14豊島郡ノ6|chapter=徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}}} ==関連項目== * [[志多羅神上洛事件]]
{{日本の伝統芸能}} {{日本の文化財}} {{デフォルトソート:たあそひ}} [[Category:稲作]] [[Category:神道]] [[Category:予祝芸能]] [[Category:新春の季語]]

'''田遊び'''（たあそび）は、模擬的な[[稲作]]所作を行う日本の[[民俗芸能]]。
== 概要 == 田遊びは、稲作の作業次第を模擬的に演じてその年の[[豊作]]を祈願する[[予祝芸能|予祝]]行事で、[[正月|新春]]に行われる場合が多い。田遊びという呼称は主に[[東海地方]]などで使用され、[[御田]]・[[御田植祭]]・春鍬・春田打ち・田祭り<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html 鶴見の歴史 第5回:鶴見の伝統芸能（その2）鶴見の田祭り]（鶴見区役所）</ref>などとも称する。地方によって違いはあるが、中心は種まきから刈取りまでを演じる。[[新井恒易]]によれば、全国で300余りの伝承があるという。

== 代表的な田遊び == * [[東京都]][[板橋区]]に伝わる[[重要無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[板橋の田遊び]]。 * [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]][[鶴見中央]]に伝わる[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市登録地域無形民俗文化財]]登録の民俗芸能、[[鶴見神社 (横浜市)|鶴見の田祭り]]<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf 市域文化財一覧（PDF版）]（横浜市）</ref>。 * [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]藤守に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[藤守の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[牧之原市]]蛭ヶ谷の[[蛭児神社 (牧之原市)|蛭児神社]]に伝わる重要無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[蛭ヶ谷の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[藤枝市]]の滝沢八坂神社に伝わる[[静岡県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|静岡県の無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[滝沢の田遊び]]。 * 静岡県[[三島市]]の[[三嶋大社]]に伝わる静岡県の無形民俗文化財の民俗芸能、[[三嶋大社のお田打]]。 * 静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]一宮の[[小國神社]]に伝わる[[選択無形民俗文化財]]の民俗芸能、[[小國神社の田遊び]]。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『郷土史大辞典 下』（朝倉書店、2005年）ISBN 4-254-53013-7、1043頁 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = [[星野紘]] |title = 音楽的呪から身体演技へ |journal = 身ぶりと音楽 |date = 1990 |publisher = 東京書籍 |series = 民族音楽叢書 |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ14豊島郡ノ6|chapter=徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 徳丸本村／徳丸脇村 天神社}}}} ==関連項目== * [[志多羅神上洛事件]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11577415]] Wikidata name: ta-asobi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual performance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|qq=|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/folk performing arts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 芸能|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a [[:en:Ritual|ritual]] performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a [[:en:Bumper crop|bountiful harvest]]. It is often held during the [[:en:Japanese New Year|Japanese New Year]] period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkuwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Uchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamasuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunehisa Arai|qq=|1=Tsuneyasu Arai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501176}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving [[:en:Cattle|oxen]] (or [[:en:Horse|horses]]) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plows|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラウ|1=plough|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land preparation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田打ち|1=land preparation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576807}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/puddling|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲作}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Nobuo Orikuchi]], in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice field deities]], offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=|1=Kitano Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|qq=|1=Tokumaru, Itabashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on [[:en:February 21|February 21]] is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|qq=|1=taue odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku]].
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabashi Ta-asobi|qq=|1=Itabashi Ta-asobi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532640}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of [[:en:Itabashi|Itabashi Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|qq=|1=Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928910}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Ward|qq=|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]].<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in [[:en:Yaizu|Yaizu City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|qq=|1=Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625585}} in [[:en:Makinohara|Makinohara City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Taisha]] in [[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima City]], Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Okuni Shrine|lt=Okuni Shrine|lt=Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小国神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Town, Shuchi District|qq=|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* "Regional Folklore Encyclopedia, Vol. 2" (Asakura Publishing, 2005), ISBN 4-254-53013-7, p. 1043 * {{Cite journal |和書 |author = {{ill|Hoshino Hiroshi|ja|星野紘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshino Hiroshi|qq=|1=Hiroshi Hoshino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513170}} |title = From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances |journal = Gestures and Music |date = 1990 |publisher = Tokyo Shoseki |series = Ethnomusicology Series |isbn = 4487752590 |ref = harv }} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki}}}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitara Incident|ja|志多羅神上洛事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitara Incident|qq=|1=Shitara Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119864321}}

It is often held during the [[:en:Japanese New Year|Japanese New Year]] period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkuwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Uchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamasuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunehisa Arai|qq=|1=Tsuneyasu Arai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501176}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving [[:en:Cattle|oxen]] (or [[:en:Horse|horses]]) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plows|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラウ|1=plough|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land preparation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田打ち|1=land preparation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576807}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/puddling|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲作}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Nobuo Orikuchi]], in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice field deities]], offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=|1=Kitano Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|qq=|1=Tokumaru, Itabashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on [[:en:February 21|February 21]] is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|qq=|1=taue odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku]].
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabashi Ta-asobi|qq=|1=Itabashi Ta-asobi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532640}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of [[:en:Itabashi|Itabashi Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|qq=|1=Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928910}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Ward|qq=|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]].<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in [[:en:Yaizu|Yaizu City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|qq=|1=Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625585}} in [[:en:Makinohara|Makinohara City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Taisha]] in [[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima City]], Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Okuni Shrine|lt=Okuni Shrine|lt=Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小国神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi <!--Ta-asobi--> [[Category:Pre-celebration Performing Arts]] [[Category:Seasonal terms for New Year]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11577415}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11577415]] Wikidata name: ta-asobi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual performance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/folk performing arts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 芸能|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a [[:en:Ritual|ritual]] performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a [[:en:Bumper crop|bountiful harvest]]. It is often held during the [[:en:Japanese New Year|Japanese New Year]] period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkuwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Uchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamasuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunehisa Arai|1=Tsuneyasu Arai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501176}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving [[:en:Cattle|oxen]] (or [[:en:Horse|horses]]) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラウ|1=plough|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land preparation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田打ち|1=land preparation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576807}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/puddling|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲作}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Nobuo Orikuchi]], in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the [[:en:Ta-no-Kami|rice field deities]], offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|1=Kitano Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|1=Tokumaru, Itabashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on [[:en:February 21|February 21]] is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|1=taue odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tohoku]].

Ward|ja|鶴見区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Ward|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]].<ref Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in [[:en:Yaizu|Yaizu City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|1=Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625585}} in [[:en:Makinohara|Makinohara City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at [[:en:Mishima Taisha|Mishima Taisha]] in [[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima City]], Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Okuni Shrine|lt=Okuni Shrine|lt=Oguni Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Okuni Shrine|lt=Okuni Shrine|lt=Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小国神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Town, Shuchi District|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}}.
