One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|1=taue odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabashi Ta-asobi|1=Itabashi Ta-asobi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532640}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|WD=Q232635|lt=Itabashi Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|1=Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928910}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Ward|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu|en|Yaizu|WD=Q696783|lt=Yaizu City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|1=Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|comment=changed Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda Taisha|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Taisha}} in {{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|en|Mishima, Shizuoka|WD=Q653478|lt=Mishima City}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]]
'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual performance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=rice farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/folk performing arts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 芸能|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|WD=Q189819|lt=ritual}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest}}. It is often held during the {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=Japanese New Year}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkuwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Uchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamasuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunehisa Arai|1=Tsuneyasu Arai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501176}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|Cattle|en|Cattle|WD=Q830|lt=oxen}} (or {{ill|Horse|en|Horse|WD=Q726|lt=horses}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラウ|1=plough|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land preparation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田打ち|1=land preparation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576807}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/puddling|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲作}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|en|Shinobu Orikuchi|WD=Q3482286|lt=Nobuo Orikuchi}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice field deities}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|1=Kitano Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|1=Tokumaru, Itabashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|1=taue odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabashi Ta-asobi|1=Itabashi Ta-asobi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532640}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|WD=Q232635|lt=Itabashi Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|1=Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928910}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Ward|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu|en|Yaizu|WD=Q696783|lt=Yaizu City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|1=Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625585}} in {{ill|Makinohara|en|Makinohara|WD=Q850527|lt=Makinohara City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|en|Mishima Taisha|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Taisha}} in {{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|en|Mishima, Shizuoka|WD=Q653478|lt=Mishima City}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Okuni Shrine|lt=Okuni Shrine|lt=Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小国神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Town, Shuchi District|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}}.

For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|1=Kitano Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|1=Tokumaru, Itabashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|1=taue odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==

of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|WD=Q232635|lt=Itabashi Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]]
'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual performance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/folk performing arts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 芸能|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|WD=Q189819|lt=ritual|1=Ritual}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest|1=Bumper crop}}. It is often held during the {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=Japanese New Year|1=Japanese New Year}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkuwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Uchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamasuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunehisa Arai|1=Tsuneyasu Arai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501176}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|Cattle|en|Cattle|WD=Q830|lt=oxen|1=Cattle}} (or {{ill|Horse|en|Horse|WD=Q726|lt=horses|1=Horse}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラウ|1=Plough|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land preparation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田打ち|1=Q11576807|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576807}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/puddling|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲作}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|en|Shinobu Orikuchi|WD=Q3482286|lt=Nobuo Orikuchi|1=Shinobu Orikuchi}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice field deities|1=Ta-no-Kami}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|1=Kitano Tenjinsha (Tokorozawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|1=Q125246588|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21|1=February 21}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|1=Rice-planting dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku|1=Tōhoku region}}.
== Notable Examples of Ta-asobi ==
* The {{ill|Itabashi Ta-asobi|ja|板橋の田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itabashi Ta-asobi|1=Q11532640|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532640}}, a {{ill|Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} of {{ill|Itabashi|en|Itabashi|WD=Q232635|lt=Itabashi Ward|1=Itabashi}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. * The "Tsurumi Ta-asobi," a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Regional Cultural Property|1=Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928910}} performed at {{ill|Tsurumi Shrine|ja|鶴見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsurumi Ward|ja|鶴見区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumi Ward|1=Tsurumi Ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840784}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}.<ref name=Tsurumi/><ref>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf Yokohama City Cultural Property List (PDF)]</ref> * The "Fujimori Ta-asobi," a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property in {{ill|Yaizu|en|Yaizu|WD=Q696783|lt=Yaizu City|1=Yaizu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. * The "Hirugaya Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|ja|蛭児神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko Shrine (Makinohara)|1=Q11625585|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11625585}} in {{ill|Makinohara|en|Makinohara|WD=Q850527|lt=Makinohara City|1=Makinohara}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, also a Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. * The "Takizawa Ta-asobi," performed at Takizawa Yasaka Shrine in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Mishima Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Mishima Taisha|en|Mishima Taisha|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Taisha|1=Mishima Taisha}} in {{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|en|Mishima, Shizuoka|WD=Q653478|lt=Mishima City|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture, also an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Shizuoka Prefecture. * The "Oguni Shrine Ta-asobi," performed at {{ill|Okuni Shrine|lt=Okuni Shrine|lt=Oguni Shrine|ja|小國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小国神社}} in {{ill|Mori Town, Shuchi District|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Town, Shuchi District|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q854613}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}}.

Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|en|Shinobu Orikuchi|WD=Q3482286|lt=Nobuo Orikuchi|1=Shinobu Orikuchi}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice field deities|1=Ta-no-Kami}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|1=Kitano Tenjinsha (Tokorozawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|1=Q125246588|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21|1=February 21}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:田遊び]]
'''Ta-asobi''' ({{ill|Ta-asobi|ja|田遊び|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-asobi|qq=}}) is a form of {{ill|ritual performance|ja|儀礼的な演技|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual performance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Japan that mimics {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice cultivation|1=Rice Cultivation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734080}} practices as part of its {{ill|folk performing arts|ja|民俗芸能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/folk performing arts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 芸能|1=performing arts in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615913}} traditions.
== Overview ==
Ta-asobi is a {{ill|Ritual|en|Ritual|WD=Q189819|lt=ritual|1=Ritual}} performance where participants simulate the various stages of rice cultivation to pray for a {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|WD=Q4997332|lt=bountiful harvest|1=Bumper crop}}. It is often held during the {{ill|Japanese New Year|en|Japanese New Year|WD=Q701398|lt=Japanese New Year|1=Japanese New Year}} period and is known by different names in various regions, such as {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田|1=Sacred rice field|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred rice field|qq=}}, spring plowing ({{ill|Shunkuwa|ja|春鍬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunkuwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), spring rice pounding ({{ill|Haruta Uchi|ja|春田打ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruta Uchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or rice festival ({{ill|Tamasuri|ja|田祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamasuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name=Tsurumi>[https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/tsurumi/shokai/gaiyo/rekishi/5_tsurumi/no05-2.html History of Tsurumi No.5: Traditional Arts of Tsurumi, Part 2: Tsurumi’s Rice Festival] (Tsurumi Ward Office)</ref> While details differ regionally, the core ritual often dramatizes the process of sowing seeds to harvesting rice. According to {{ill|Tsunehisa Arai|ja|新井恒易|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunehisa Arai|1=Tsuneyasu Arai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501176}}, over 300 such traditions exist across Japan.
In many Ta-asobi rituals, scenes involving {{ill|Cattle|en|Cattle|WD=Q830|lt=oxen|1=Cattle}} (or {{ill|Horse|en|Horse|WD=Q726|lt=horses|1=Horse}}) used for cultivation are key highlights.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=Hoshino Hiroshi|year=1990|title=From Musical Rituals to Bodily Performances|journal=Gestures and Music|publisher=Tokyo Shoseki|pages=199–208}}</ref> Participants often wear a canopy (horokasa) to impersonate oxen, pulling {{ill|plows|ja|犂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/plows|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to プラウ|1=Plough|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464}} and mimicking {{ill|land preparation|ja|田起こし|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land preparation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田打ち|1=Q11576807|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576807}} and {{ill|puddling|ja|代かき|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/puddling|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲作}}. In some performances, oxen are represented by dolls or other substitutes. Variations include unruly oxen scenes or rituals praising and honoring the oxen. {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|en|Shinobu Orikuchi|WD=Q3482286|lt=Nobuo Orikuchi|1=Shinobu Orikuchi}}, in his essay "The Concept of Ta-asobi Rituals," speculated that the oxen might have originally symbolized sacrifices to the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|en|Ta-no-Kami|WD=Q7683716|lt=rice field deities|1=Ta-no-Kami}}, offered to ensure abundant harvests.
== Examples ==
For example, at {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|1=Kitano Tenjinsha (Tokorozawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404275}} in {{ill|Tokumaru, Itabashi|ja|徳丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokumaru, Itabashi|1=Q125246588|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125246588}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, the annual "Tokumaru Ta-asobi" held on {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|WD=Q2350|lt=February 21|1=February 21}} is described in the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}} as a sacred ritual referred to as "Ta-asobi Festival." During the event, participants enact rice cultivation processes in a two-ken-square space called a {{ill|mogari|ja|モガリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mogari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} within the shrine precinct. One notable act involves "calling in," during which the farmer characters Taroji and Anjo perform a dance symbolizing union and {{ill|fertility ritual|ja|性的儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fertility ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to evoke abundant harvests.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki|volume=Volume 14, Toshima District, Section 6|chapter=Tokumaru Motomura / Tokumaru Wakimura, Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763977/56}}}}</ref>
Other related events include {{ill|rice planting dances|ja|田植踊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rice planting dances|1=Rice-planting dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577057}} and regional performances like {{ill|Hachinohe Enburi|ja|八戸のえんぶり|1=Enburi|2=ja|3=えんぶり|lt=Hachinohe Enburi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachinohe Enburi|13=User:Immanuelle/Enburi|qq=}} in {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tohoku|1=Tōhoku region}}.
